data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S6 1ba5: Docs — private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model)]

4-bucket audit of this Docs child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE 8-child roster (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache, S2b b73c janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate ref-coherence, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing). This child is the DOCUMENTATION slice. The union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. EMPTY G3 findings list.

Coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this docs child's role):
- AC1 (no attested gate reads the working tree; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY by S1 (6d56 AC1/AC2 faithful read-tree+checkout-index) + S3 (fa49 AC1 re-rooting). This child DOCUMENTS the attested/ref semantics (its AC3). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this docs child (Scope: "Out: code (S1-S5)"). Not an absence.
- AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the signed manifest channel — no payload-schema/version change, prior signatures verify — reproducible against that SHA) — FULLY by S4 (30b3 AC1: verified-at-sha manifest step, no PAYLOAD_VERSION bump, prior sigs verify; AC2: reproducible) atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. This child DOCUMENTS the signing/verified_at_sha semantics (its AC3) + HMAC trust model + in-toto Statement shape (its AC4), explicitly delegated by S4 ("its limits ... DOCUMENTED in S6"). Behavior owned by S4; docs half here. Not an absence.
- AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict; source=local is the documented back-out restoring prior in-place behavior) — by S3 (fa49 AC4 reads in-place + its advisory AC "source=local documented as rollback to in-place reads") + S4 (30b3 AC3 bars signing). This child DOCUMENTS attested-signs/local-never-signs + fail-closed-attested/local-still-runs (its AC2/AC3). The "documented back-out" facet has a behavioral home in S3 and a prose home here. Not an absence.
- AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark — S2 — without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY by S1 (atomic population, no index.lock/config.lock contention) + S2 (70c7 single-flight + delete-on-last-close) + S2b (b73c janitor reclaim under watermark). This child DOCUMENTS the disk-cap behavior (its AC5). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. the mechanism. Not an absence.
- AC5 (private-repo fetch failures surface a descriptive, actionable credential error) — SPLIT: S1 (6d56 AC5) RAISES the actionable error; THIS child is the DOCS PRIMARY OWNER (its AC1: README MCP-setup + docs/jira-sync-setup.md state the fetch-credential requirement + how to supply it, grep-able; its AC2: message + remedy + fail-closed-attested/local-still-runs in a named file). The "actionable"/how-to-supply facet is precisely this child's contribution. Clean split.
- AC6 (portable across macOS + Linux: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — implementation FULLY by S1 (6d56 AC4 + portability seam). This child DOCUMENTS REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR (no hardcoded /tmp) + the EFS/NFS flock caveat (its AC5, xref alto-fruit-punch). Clean impl/docs split. Not an absence.
- AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults, S2/S3) — implementation by S2b (b73c AC9: watermark, grace window, max-age, reverify period configurable with documented defaults) + S3 (fa49 AC: ref/source defaults via standard rebar config + REBAR_* precedence). This child DOCUMENTS the disk-cap/knobs (its AC5) + the origin/main default + ref/source semantics (its AC3). The "documented defaults" facet is co-owned (each tunable child carries its own documented-defaults AC; this child carries the prose). Not an absence.
- AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives — PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — authored by S2/S2b (b73c AC10: ADR records cache/janitor architecture + rejected PID+heartbeat lease) and S4 (30b3 AC1: manifest-vs-payload SHA-binding decision recorded in the ADR — covering the rejected payload-schema SHA-binding alternative). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this docs child (the ADR is authored in S2/S4, not this prose-docs child; its Scope: "Out: code (S1-S5)"). Both rejected alternatives the parent names have an authoring home. Not an absence.
- AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children in the roster (this child is the docs story, 1 of 8) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC6. This child carries the operator/trust-model DOCUMENTATION surface the snapshot model newly requires (the new fetch-credential prerequisite, the descriptive failure behavior, the attested-signs/local-never-signs + origin/main + verified_at_sha semantics, the HMAC trust model + limits + in-toto Statement shape, REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR/disk-cap/EFS-NFS-flock knobs). It introduces no behavioral obligation of its own and leaves no clause uncovered.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences):
- S4 (30b3) "What"/AC5 explicitly delegates the HMAC-trust-model + in-toto Statement-shape docs to S6 — claimed here by this child's AC4.
- S1 (6d56 AC5) defers the descriptive credential MESSAGE detail to S6 ("see S6") — claimed here by this child's AC1/AC2.
- S5 (b7fa) scopes prose docs out ("Out: ... prose docs (S6)"); the ref/source PROSE semantics are claimed here by AC3 (schema-text vs prose split clean — S5 owns inline outputSchema/text, this child owns the human prose in named files).
Each cross-ref resolves to a concrete AC on this child; no docs obligation named by a sibling is left unclaimed.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on sibling S4 (30b3) about routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check (and the related S4b e308 ref-coherence seam) is a CONSISTENCY (G4) code seam between S3/S4/S4b; it does not touch this docs child and leaves no parent criterion unclaimed (AC1/AC2/ref-coherence remain claimed by children).

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the documentation slice of the parent criteria — it is the docs primary owner of parent AC5 (credential requirement + actionable remedy, grep-able) and the docs half of AC2/AC3/AC4/AC6/AC7. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
